verification_gate: Entity counts, identities, bytes, metadata, relationships, permissions, history, semantic retrieval, behavior, reconstruction, backup, restore, failback, and observed LifeOS operation all pass before one explicit authority switch; preserved source remains available until retention approval.

gate_result.entity_counts_check: PD-01: per-class COUNT(*) of PostgreSQL rows equals the POSTGRES-004 source-inventory count; pass = exact per-class equality across all 14 switching classes.
gate_result.identities_check: PD-02: every source stable identity (SHA-256 blob hash, module_path, task/anchor/proof/edge id) maps bijectively to one PostgreSQL identity and back; pass = zero orphans, zero collisions.
gate_result.bytes_check: PD-03: SHA-256 of every PostgreSQL-stored blob equals the source blob under the BlobStore parity contract; evidence = green live blobstore_parity.rs against a disposable PostgreSQL; pass = all content hashes equal.
gate_result.metadata_check: PD-04: mode/permission bits, timestamps, size, mime, module_path, and ordering preserved via the POSTGRES-004 content-and-metadata checksum; pass = metadata checksum equal per entity.
gate_result.relationships_check: PD-05: containment/dependency/causal edges reconstructed in PostgreSQL+RuVector match the source-derived edge set by endpoints and type; pass = edge-set equality, no dangling or invented edges.
gate_result.permissions_check: PD-06: permission/ACL records reconstruct identical access decisions over a principal x resource matrix; pass = decision diff empty.
gate_result.history_check: PD-07: temporal/provenance history preserved and PostgreSQL PITR reconstructs any prior committed state; source commit/PR lineage retained; pass = history depth equals source and PITR restore reproduces the target state.
gate_result.semantic_retrieval_check: PD-08: RuVector hybrid search (ORDER BY semantic_embedding <->) returns the same top-k targets as a reference query set and re-embedding is reproducible; pass = top-k parity within tolerance; gated on live RuVector-in-PostgreSQL activation.
gate_result.behavior_check: PD-09: LifeOS behavior driven from PostgreSQL equals flat-file-authoritative behavior for the bounded slice (extends the POSTGRES-009 round trip); pass = behavior diff empty.
gate_result.reconstruction_check: PD-10: envctl projection from PostgreSQL reproduces a byte-identical source directory (POSTGRES-008 determinism); pass = projected-tree SHA-256 equals source and repeated projections are byte-identical.
gate_result.backup_check: PD-11: a full checksummed backup of the PostgreSQL authority is captured before Stage 4; pass = backup exists with recorded checksum.
gate_result.restore_check: PD-12: restoring that backup into a disposable namespace yields identical state; pass = restored-state checksum equals source checksum.
gate_result.failback_check: PD-13: rehearsed failback to preserved source authority stops database writes with no data loss and both representations intact; pass = failback rehearsal green, zero unarchived destructive action.
gate_result.observed_lifeos_operation_check: PD-14: LifeOS Mission Control answers a UDS request from the canonical PostgreSQL state with an audit event (POSTGRES-007 path); pass = UDS round trip answered from canonical DB, audit event recorded, no hidden flat-file control-plane detour.
gate_result.one_explicit_authority_switch: Stage 4 only, owner-approved, gated on PD-01..PD-14 all green plus the STORE-001 verified-cutover invariant; effect = editable authority moves flat-file -> PostgreSQL, flat files become read-only preserved reference + envctl-derived projection.
gate_result.source_deletion_or_history_loss_or_irrev: none — forbidden by blocked_paths; failback retains both representations and every superseded namespace.
gate_result.store_001_alignment: Single durable writer PostgreSQL; forward-only redb -> envctl -> PostgreSQL sync; verified per-class cutover under one SHA-256 identity via the BlobStore parity contract; retiring flat-file editable authority is building-to-spec (upgrade, not downgrade) because the envctl projection reproduces byte-identical source and the preserved source is retained until retention approval.
open_dependencies: Live RuVector-in-PostgreSQL extension activation required before the semantic-embeddings class fully cuts over (STORE-001 open dependency; NBVERIFY-001 / REDB-CLAIM-023 remainder).
Live blobstore_parity.rs green run against a disposable PostgreSQL captures the durable-class cutover proof (STORE-001 open dependency).
gate_result.authority_state_after_this_task: unchanged — no switch performed (next_action: keep authority unchanged)

proof_summary: POSTGRES-010 defines the staged whole-user-space parity recovery and authority cutover as planning artifacts only; no database was provisioned or mutated and no authority was switched. generated/postgres_authority_cutover_plan.json enumerates the entire user-space as 14 switching classes drawn from the POSTGRES-002 model gate and the STORE-001 state-class decision (USC-FILES, USC-DIRECTORIES, USC-CODE-MODULES, USC-CONFIG, USC-PLANNING, USC-COMMUNICATIONS, USC-APP-STATE, USC-MEDIA-BLOBREFS, USC-IDENTITIES, USC-PERMISSIONS, USC-HISTORY, USC-RELATIONSHIPS, USC-SEMANTIC-EMBEDDINGS, USC-ENVCTL-MIGRATION-STATE) plus 4 non-switching classes that retain their STORE-001 owner (transient redb, per-agent AgentDB cognition, envctl/CodeDB derived projections, and OS-keyring external secrets). Every class is assigned exactly one canonical owner and one authority_action, and the two current durable redb uses (PGREDB-001 CodeDB source_blobs and PGREDB-002 envctl migration_db) are named explicitly so neither is silently reclassified — they migrate only through the BlobStore SHA-256 verified-cutover contract. Each of the 14 gate clauses is bound to a concrete parity dimension PD-01..PD-14 with a check, evidence, and pass criterion. The plan sequences five stages (parity decomposition, restore rehearsal, staged dual-read, explicit switch, retention decision) and permits exactly one explicit owner-approved authority switch (Stage 4) that fires only after all 14 dimensions pass and the STORE-001 verified-cutover invariant holds; at switch, flat files stop being editable authority and become the read-only preserved reference plus the byte-identical envctl projection. Retiring flat-file authority is an upgrade to the intended blueprint spec, preserved via the BlobStore parity mechanism and the deterministic projection path, with the source retained until a separate retention approval. The rollback fails back to preserved source authority, stops database writes, and retains the failed cutover namespace and proof without deleting either representation. Authority is left unchanged; product execution remains closed.
